content.text:
I'll analyze the current state of the MFC (MMS - Metro Mileage Management System) project to understand its structure and provide a summary.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && find . -type f -name "*.md" | head -20</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user said "Warmup" - this seems like they want me to get familiar with the codebase. Let me explore the project structure and understand what's here.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">**/*</parameter>
<parameter name="limit">100</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && jq '.value | length' db.json 2>/dev/null || echo "db.json not found or invalid"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && jq '[.value[] | .status] | group_by(.) | map({status: .[0], count: length})' db.json 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la _bmad/ 2>/dev/null || echo "_bmad directory not found"</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/_bmad/bmm-workflow-status.yaml</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la _bmad/bmm/agents/ 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la _bmad/custom/agents/ 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/_bmad/project-context.md</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la bmad-artifacts/ 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la bmad-knowledge/ 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC && ls -la docs/ 2>/dev/null</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

I've completed the warmup analysis of the MFC (MMS - Metro Mileage Management System) project. Here's a summary:

## Project Overview

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** - A railway mileage optimization system using the BMAD Method V6.

### Current Status

| Status | Count |
|--------|-------|
| TODO | 13 tasks |
| DONE | 6 tasks |

**Current Phase:** Phase 1 (Analysis) - In Progress

### Project Structure

```
MFC/
├── CLAUDE.md              # AI instructions
├── db.json                # Task tracking (19 total tasks)
├── _bmad/                 # BMAD framework
│   ├── bmm/agents/        # 8 standard agents
│   ├── custom/agents/     # 4 MMS-specific agents
│   ├── project-context.md # Project configuration
│   └── bmm-workflow-status.yaml
├── bmad-artifacts/        # Phase deliverables (phases 1-4)
├── bmad-knowledge/        # Domain knowledge (business, domain, technical)
└── docs/                  # Project documentation
```

### BMAD Agents Available

**Standard Agents (8):**
- analyst.agent.md (Alex)
- architect.agent.md (Sofia)
- dev.agent.md
- pm.agent.md (John)
- po.agent.md
- qa.agent.md
- sm.agent.md (Marcus)
- ux-designer.agent.md (Uma)

**Custom Agents (4):**
- maria-railway-domain-expert.agent.md
- otto-or-specialist.agent.md
- paulo-railway-ops.agent.md
- tiago-metro-sp-engineer.agent.md

### Key Configuration

- **Methodology:** BMAD V6
- **Language:** English for code/docs, Portuguese (pt-BR) for user communication
- **Commit discipline:** One commit per completed task
- **Task execution:** Via subagents (executor + monitor pattern)

The project is ready for continuing tasks. Use `continue` to resume pending tasks or `status` for more details.
